NKJV.1: And He looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the treasury,
NKJV.2: and He saw also a certain poor widow putting in two mites.
NKJV.3: So He said, “Truly I say to you  that this poor widow has put in more than all;
NKJV.4: for all these out of their abundance have put in offerings for God, but she out of her poverty put in  all the livelihood that she had.”
NKJV.5: Then, as some spoke of the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and donations, He said,
NKJV.6: “These things which you see—the days will come in which not one stone shall be left upon another that shall not be thrown down.”
NKJV.7: So they asked Him, saying, “Teacher, but when will these things be? And what sign will there be when these things are about to take place?”
NKJV.8: And He said: “Take heed that you not be deceived. For many will come in My name, saying, ‘I am He,’ and, ‘The time has drawn near.’ Therefore do not go after them.
NKJV.9: But when you hear of wars and commotions, do not be terrified; for these things must come to pass first, but the end will not come immediately.”
NKJV.10: Then He said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
NKJV.11: And there will be great  earthquakes in various places, and famines and pestilences; and there will be fearful sights and great signs from heaven.
NKJV.12: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues and prisons. You will be brought before kings and rulers for My name’s sake.
NKJV.13: But  it will turn out for you as an occasion for testimony.
NKJV.14: Therefore settle it in your hearts not to meditate beforehand on what you will answer;
NKJV.15: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom  which all your adversaries will not be able to contradict or resist.
NKJV.16: You will be betrayed even by parents and brothers, relatives and friends; and they will put some of you to death.
NKJV.17: And  you will be hated by all for My name’s sake.
NKJV.18: But not a hair of your head shall be lost.
NKJV.19: By your patience possess your souls.
NKJV.20: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is near.
NKJV.21: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, let those who are in the midst of her depart, and let not those who are in the country enter her.
NKJV.22: For these are the days of vengeance, that  all things which are written may be fulfilled.
NKJV.23: But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days! For there will be great distress in the land and wrath upon this people.
NKJV.24: And they will fall by the edge of the sword, and be led away captive into all nations. And Jerusalem will be trampled by Gentiles  until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
NKJV.25: “And there will be signs in the sun, in the moon, and in the stars; and on the earth distress of nations, with perplexity, the sea and the waves roaring;
NKJV.26: men’s hearts failing them from fear and the expectation of those things which are coming on the earth,  for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
NKJV.27: Then they will see the Son of Man  coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
NKJV.28: Now when these things begin to happen, look up and lift up your heads, because  your redemption draws near.”
NKJV.29: Then He spoke to them a parable: “Look at the fig tree, and all the trees.
NKJV.30: When they are already budding, you see and know for yourselves that summer is now near.
NKJV.31: So you also, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
NKJV.32: Assuredly, I say to you, this generation will by no means pass away till all things take place.
NKJV.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My  words will by no means pass away.
NKJV.34: “But  take heed to yourselves, lest your hearts be weighed down with carousing, drunkenness, and  cares of this life, and that Day come on you unexpectedly.
NKJV.35: For  it will come as a snare on all those who dwell on the face of the whole earth.
NKJV.36: Watch therefore, and  pray always that you may be counted  worthy to escape all these things that will come to pass, and  to stand before the Son of Man.”
NKJV.37: And in the daytime He was teaching in the temple, but at night He went out and stayed on the mountain called Olivet.
NKJV.38: Then early in the morning all the people came to Him in the temple to hear Him.

ESV.1: Jesus  looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the offering box,
ESV.2: and he saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.
ESV.3: And he said, “Truly, I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all of them.
ESV.4: For they all contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on.”
ESV.5: And while some were speaking of the temple, how it was adorned with noble stones and offerings, he said,
ESV.6: “As for these things that you see, the days will come when there will not be left here one stone upon another that will not be thrown down.”
ESV.7: And they asked him, “Teacher, when will these things be, and what will be the sign when these things are about to take place?”
ESV.8: And he said,  “See that you are not led astray. For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’ and, ‘The time is at hand!’ Do not go after them.
ESV.9: And when you hear of wars and tumults, do not be terrified, for these things must first take place, but the end will not be at once.”
ESV.10: Then he said to them,  “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
ESV.11: There will be great earthquakes, and in various places famines and pestilences. And there will be terrors and great signs from heaven.
ESV.12: But before all this they will lay their hands on you and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors for my name's sake.
ESV.13: This will be your opportunity to bear witness.
ESV.14: Settle it therefore in your minds not to meditate beforehand how to answer,
ESV.15: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which none of your adversaries will be able to withstand or contradict.
ESV.16: You will be delivered up even by parents and brothers  and relatives and friends, and some of you they will put to death.
ESV.17: You will be hated by all for my name's sake.
ESV.18: But not a hair of your head will perish.
ESV.19: By your endurance you will gain your lives.
ESV.20: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.
ESV.21: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, and let those who are inside the city depart, and let not those who are out in the country enter it,
ESV.22: for these are days of vengeance, to fulfill all that is written.
ESV.23: Alas for women who are pregnant and for those who are nursing infants in those days! For there will be great distress upon the earth and wrath against this people.
ESV.24: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be led captive among all nations, and Jerusalem will be trampled underfoot by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
ESV.25: “And there will be signs in sun and moon and stars, and on the earth distress of nations in perplexity because of the roaring of the sea and the waves,
ESV.26: people fainting with fear and with foreboding of what is coming on the world. For the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
ESV.27: And then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
ESV.28: Now when these things begin to take place, straighten up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
ESV.29: And he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree, and all the trees.
ESV.30: As soon as they come out in leaf, you see for yourselves and know that the summer is already near.
ESV.31: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that the kingdom of God is near.
ESV.32: Truly, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all has taken place.
ESV.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
ESV.34: “But watch yourselves lest your hearts be weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and cares of this life, and that day come upon you suddenly like a trap.
ESV.35: For it will come upon all who dwell on the face of the whole earth.
ESV.36: But stay awake at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are going to take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
ESV.37: And every day he was teaching in the temple, but at night he went out and lodged on the mount called Olivet.
ESV.38: And early in the morning all the people came to him in the temple to hear him.

NASB.1: And He looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the treasury.
NASB.2: And He saw a poor widow putting in two small copper coins.
NASB.3: And He said, 'Truly I say to you, this poor widow put in more than all [of them];
NASB.4: for they all out of their surplus put into the offering; but she out of her poverty put in all that she had to live on.'
NASB.5: And while some were talking about the temple, that it was adorned with beautiful stones and votive gifts, He said,
NASB.6: '[As for] these things which you are looking at, the days will come in which there will not be left one stone upon another which will not be torn down.'
NASB.7: They questioned Him, saying, 'Teacher, when therefore will these things happen? And what [will be] the sign when these things are about to take place?'
NASB.8: And He said, 'See to it that you are not misled; for many will come in My name, saying, 'I am [He],' and, 'The time is near.' Do not go after them.
NASB.9: 'When you hear of wars and disturbances, do not be terrified; for these things must take place first, but the end [does] not [follow] immediately.'
NASB.10: Then He continued by saying to them, 'Nation will rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom,
NASB.11: and there will be great earthquakes, and in various places plagues and famines; and there will be terrors and great signs from heaven.
NASB.12: 'But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you to the synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for My name's sake.
NASB.13: 'It will lead to an opportunity for your testimony.
NASB.14: 'So make up your minds not to prepare beforehand to defend yourselves;
NASB.15: for I will give you utterance and wisdom which none of your opponents will be able to resist or refute.
NASB.16: 'But you will be betrayed even by parents and brothers and relatives and friends, and they will put [some] of you to death,
NASB.17: and you will be hated by all because of My name.
NASB.18: 'Yet not a hair of your head will perish.
NASB.19: 'By your endurance you will gain your lives.
NASB.20: 'But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then recognize that her desolation is near.
NASB.21: 'Then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those who are in the midst of the city must leave, and those who are in the country must not enter the city;
NASB.22: because these are days of vengeance, so that all things which are written will be fulfilled.
NASB.23: 'Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days; for there will be great distress upon the land and wrath to this people;
NASB.24: and they will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled under foot by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
NASB.25: 'There will be signs in sun and moon and stars, and on the earth dismay among nations, in perplexity at the roaring of the sea and the waves,
NASB.26: men fainting from fear and the expectation of the things which are coming upon the world; for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
NASB.27: 'Then they will see THE SON OF MAN COMING IN A CLOUD with power and great glory.
NASB.28: 'But when these things begin to take place, straighten up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.'
NASB.29: Then He told them a parable: 'Behold the fig tree and all the trees;
NASB.30: as soon as they put forth [leaves], you see it and know for yourselves that summer is now near.
NASB.31: 'So you also, when you see these things happening, recognize that the kingdom of God is near.
NASB.32: 'Truly I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all things take place.
NASB.33: 'Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
NASB.34: 'Be on guard, so that your hearts will not be weighted down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of life, and that day will not come on you suddenly like a trap;
NASB.35: for it will come upon all those who dwell on the face of all the earth.
NASB.36: 'But keep on the alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that are about to take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.'
NASB.37: Now during the day He was teaching in the temple, but at evening He would go out and spend the night on the mount that is called Olivet.
NASB.38: And all the people would get up early in the morning [to come] to Him in the temple to listen to Him.

NLT.1: While Jesus was in the Temple, he watched the rich people dropping their gifts in the collection box.
NLT.2: Then a poor widow came by and dropped in two small coins.
NLT.3: “I tell you the truth,” Jesus said, “this poor widow has given more than all the rest of them.
NLT.4: For they have given a tiny part of their surplus, but she, poor as she is, has given everything she has.”
NLT.5: Some of his disciples began talking about the majestic stonework of the Temple and the memorial decorations on the walls. But Jesus said,
NLT.6: “The time is coming when all these things will be completely demolished. Not one stone will be left on top of another!”
NLT.7: “Teacher,” they asked, “when will all this happen? What sign will show us that these things are about to take place?”
NLT.8: He replied, “Don’t let anyone mislead you, for many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am the Messiah,’ and saying, ‘The time has come!’ But don’t believe them.
NLT.9: And when you hear of wars and insurrections, don’t panic. Yes, these things must take place first, but the end won’t follow immediately.”
NLT.10: Then he added, “Nation will go to war against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
NLT.11: There will be great earthquakes, and there will be famines and plagues in many lands, and there will be terrifying things and great miraculous signs from heaven.
NLT.12: “But before all this occurs, there will be a time of great persecution. You will be dragged into synagogues and prisons, and you will stand trial before kings and governors because you are my followers.
NLT.13: But this will be your opportunity to tell them about me.
NLT.14: So don’t worry in advance about how to answer the charges against you,
NLT.15: for I will give you the right words and such wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to reply or refute you!
NLT.16: Even those closest to you — your parents, brothers, relatives, and friends — will betray you. They will even kill some of you.
NLT.17: And everyone will hate you because you are my followers.
NLT.18: But not a hair of your head will perish!
NLT.19: By standing firm, you will win your souls.
NLT.20: “And when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then you will know that the time of its destruction has arrived.
NLT.21: Then those in Judea must flee to the hills. Those in Jerusalem must get out, and those out in the country should not return to the city.
NLT.22: For those will be days of God’s vengeance, and the prophetic words of the Scriptures will be fulfilled.
NLT.23: How terrible it will be for pregnant women and for nursing mothers in those days. For there will be disaster in the land and great anger against this people.
NLT.24: They will be killed by the sword or sent away as captives to all the nations of the world. And Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles until the period of the Gentiles comes to an end.
NLT.25: “And there will be strange signs in the sun, moon, and stars. And here on earth the nations will be in turmoil, perplexed by the roaring seas and strange tides.
NLT.26: People will be terrified at what they see coming upon the earth, for the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
NLT.27: Then everyone will see the Son of Man coming on a cloud with power and great glory.
NLT.28: So when all these things begin to happen, stand and look up, for your salvation is near!”
NLT.29: Then he gave them this illustration: “Notice the fig tree, or any other tree.
NLT.30: When the leaves come out, you know without being told that summer is near.
NLT.31: In the same way, when you see all these things taking place, you can know that the Kingdom of God is near.
NLT.32: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass from the scene until all these things have taken place.
NLT.33: Heaven and earth will disappear, but my words will never disappear.
NLT.34: “Watch out! Don’t let your hearts be dulled by carousing and drunkenness, and by the worries of this life. Don’t let that day catch you unaware,
NLT.35: like a trap. For that day will come upon everyone living on the earth.
NLT.36: Keep alert at all times. And pray that you might be strong enough to escape these coming horrors and stand before the Son of Man.”
NLT.37: Every day Jesus went to the Temple to teach, and each evening he returned to spend the night on the Mount of Olives.
NLT.38: The crowds gathered at the Temple early each morning to hear him.

WEB.1: He looked up, and saw the rich people who were putting their gifts into the treasury.
WEB.2: He saw a certain poor widow casting in two small brass coins.
WEB.3: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow put in more than all of them,
WEB.4: for all these put in gifts for God from their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, put in all that she had to live on.”
WEB.5: As some were talking about the temple and how it was decorated with beautiful stones and gifts, he said,
WEB.6: “As for these things which you see, the days will come, in which there will not be left here one stone on another that will not be thrown down.”
WEB.7: They asked him, “Teacher, so when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are about to happen?”
WEB.8: He said, “Watch out that you don’t get led astray, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he ,’ and, ‘The time is at hand.’ Therefore don’t follow them.
WEB.9: When you hear of wars and disturbances, don’t be terrified, for these things must happen first, but the end won’t come immediately.”
WEB.10: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
WEB.11: There will be great earthquakes, famines, and plagues in various places. There will be terrors and great signs from heaven.
WEB.12: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, delivering you up to synagogues and prisons, bringing you before kings and governors for my name’s sake.
WEB.13: It will turn out as a testimony for you.
WEB.14: Settle it therefore in your hearts not to meditate beforehand how to answer,
WEB.15: for I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your adversaries will not be able to withstand or to contradict.
WEB.16: You will be handed over even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. They will cause some of you to be put to death.
WEB.17: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake.
WEB.18: And not a hair of your head will perish.
WEB.19: “By your endurance you will win your lives.
WEB.20: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation is at hand.
WEB.21: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let those who are in the middle of her depart. Let those who are in the country not enter therein.
WEB.22: For these are days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.
WEB.23: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who nurse infants in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.
WEB.24: They will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
WEB.25: There will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars; and on the earth anxiety of nations, in perplexity for the roaring of the sea and the waves;
WEB.26: men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
WEB.27: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
WEB.28: But when these things begin to happen, look up, and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.”
WEB.29: He told them a parable. “See the fig tree, and all the trees.
WEB.30: When they are already budding, you see it and know by your own selves that the summer is already near.
WEB.31: Even so you also, when you see these things happening, know that God’s Kingdom is near.
WEB.32: Most certainly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things are accomplished.
WEB.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will by no means pass away.
WEB.34: “So be careful, or your hearts will be loaded down with carousing, drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day will come on you suddenly.
WEB.35: For it will come like a snare on all those who dwell on the surface of all the earth.
WEB.36: Therefore be watchful all the time, praying that you may be counted worthy to escape all these things that will happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
WEB.37: Every day Jesus was teaching in the temple, and every night he would go out and spend the night on the mountain that is called Olivet.
WEB.38: All the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.

CSB17.1: He <sup>ⓕ</sup> looked up and saw the rich dropping their offerings into the temple treasury. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.2: He also saw a poor widow dropping in two tiny coins. <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.3: “Truly I tell you,” he said. “This poor widow has put in more than all of them. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.4: For all these people have put in gifts out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”
CSB17.5: As <sup>ⓙ</sup> some were talking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and gifts dedicated to God, he said,
CSB17.6: “These things that you see — the days will come <sup>ⓚ</sup>when not one stone will be left on another that will not be thrown down.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.7: “Teacher,” they asked him, “so when will these things happen? And what will be the sign when these things are about to take place?” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.8: Then he said, “Watch out that you are not deceived. <sup>ⓝ</sup>For many will come in my name, <sup>ⓐ</sup>saying, ‘I am he,’ <sup>ⓑ</sup> and, ‘The time is near.’ Don’t follow them. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.9: When you hear of wars and rebellions, <sup>[2]</sup>don’t be alarmed. Indeed, it is necessary that these things take place first, but the end won’t come right away.”
CSB17.10: Then he told them: “Nation will be raised up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.11: There will be violent earthquakes, <sup>ⓔ</sup>and famines and plagues <sup>ⓕ</sup>in various places, and there will be terrifying sights and great signs from heaven. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.12: But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you <sup>ⓗ</sup>and persecute <sup>ⓘ</sup>you. They will hand you over to the synagogues <sup>ⓙ</sup>and prisons, <sup>ⓚ</sup>and you will be brought before kings and governors <sup>ⓛ</sup>because of my name.
CSB17.13: This will give you an opportunity to bear witness. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.14: Therefore make up your minds <sup>[3]</sup>not to prepare your defense ahead of time, <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.15: for I will give you such words <sup>ⓐ</sup>and a wisdom <sup>ⓑ</sup>that none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.16: You will even be betrayed by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. <sup>ⓓ</sup>They will kill some of you.
CSB17.17: You will be hated by everyone because of my name, <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.18: but not a hair of your head will be lost. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.20: “When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, <sup>ⓗ</sup> then recognize that its desolation <sup>ⓘ</sup> has come near.
CSB17.21: Then those in Judea must flee <sup>ⓙ</sup>to the mountains. <sup>ⓚ</sup>Those inside the city must leave it, and those who are in the country must not enter it,
CSB17.22: because these are days of vengeance <sup>ⓛ</sup>to fulfill all the things that are written.
CSB17.23: Woe to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days, <sup>ⓜ</sup>for there will be great distress in the land <sup>[5]</sup>and wrath against this people.
CSB17.24: They will be killed by the sword <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup>and be led captive into all the nations, and Jerusalem will be trampled by the Gentiles <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓐ</sup>until the times of the Gentiles <sup>ⓑ</sup> are fulfilled.
CSB17.25: “Then there will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars;  <sup>ⓒ</sup> and there will be anguish on the earth among nations bewildered by the roaring of the sea and the waves. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.26: People will faint from fear and expectation of the things that are coming on the world, because the powers of the heavens will be shaken. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.27: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud <sup>ⓕ</sup>with power and great glory. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.28: But when these things begin to take place, stand up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is near.” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.29: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree, and all the trees.
CSB17.30: As soon as they put out leaves you can see for yourselves and recognize that summer is already near.
CSB17.31: In the same way, when you see these things happening, recognize <sup>[8]</sup>that the kingdom of God is near.
CSB17.32: Truly I tell you, this generation will certainly not pass away until all things take place. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, <sup>ⓙ</sup>but my words will never pass away. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.34: “Be on your guard, <sup>ⓛ</sup> so that your minds are not dulled <sup>[9]</sup> from carousing, <sup>[10]</sup> drunkenness, <sup>ⓜ</sup>and worries of life, <sup>ⓝ</sup>or that day will come on you unexpectedly <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.35: like a trap. For it will come on all who live on the face of the whole earth.
CSB17.36: But be alert at all times, <sup>ⓑ</sup>praying that you may have strength <sup>[11]</sup> to escape <sup>ⓒ</sup>all these things that are going to take place and to stand before the Son of Man.”
CSB17.37: During <sup>ⓓ</sup> the day, he was teaching in the temple, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but in the evening he would go out and spend the night on what is called the Mount of Olives. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.38: Then all the people would come early in the morning to hear him in the temple. <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Looking up, He saw the rich people putting their gifts into the treasury.
AMP.2: And He saw a poor widow putting in two small copper coins.
AMP.3: He said, "Truly I say to you, this poor widow has put in [proportionally] more than all of them;
AMP.4: for they all put in gifts from their abundance; but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on."
AMP.5: As some were talking about the temple, that it was decorated with beautiful stones and consecrated offerings [of magnificent gifts of gold which were displayed on the walls and hung in the porticoes], He said,
AMP.6: "As for all these things which you see, the time will come when there will not be one stone left on another that will not be torn down."
AMP.7: They asked Him, "Teacher, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign when these things are about to happen?"
AMP.8: He said, "Be careful and see to it that you are not misled; for many will come in My name [appropriating for themselves the name Messiah which belongs to Me alone], saying, 'I am He,' and, 'The time is near!' Do not follow them.
AMP.9: When you hear of wars and disturbances [civil unrest, revolts, uprisings], do not panic; for these things must take place first, but the end will not come immediately."
AMP.10: Then Jesus told them, "Nation will rise against nation and kingdom against kingdom.
AMP.11: There will be violent earthquakes, and in various places famines and [deadly and devastating] pestilences (plagues, epidemics); and there will be terrible sights and great signs from heaven.
AMP.12: "But before all these things, they will lay their hands on you and will persecute you, turning you over to the synagogues and prisons, and bringing you before kings and governors for My name's sake.
AMP.13: This will be a time and an opportunity for you to testify [about Me].
AMP.14: So make up your minds not to prepare beforehand to defend yourselves;
AMP.15: for I will give you [skillful] words and wisdom which none of your opponents will be able to resist or refute.
AMP.16: But you will be betrayed and handed over even by parents and brothers and relatives and friends, and they will put some of you to death,
AMP.17: and you will be continually hated by everyone because of [your association with] My name.
AMP.18: But not a hair of your head will perish.
AMP.19: By your [patient] endurance [empowered by the Holy Spirit] you will gain your souls.
AMP.20: "But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by [hostile] armies, then understand [with confident assurance] that her complete destruction is near.
AMP.21: At that time, those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those who are inside the city (Jerusalem) must get out, and those who are [out] in the country must not enter the city;
AMP.22: for these are days of vengeance [of rendering full justice or satisfaction], so that all things which are written will be fulfilled.
AMP.23: Woe to those women who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days! For great trouble and anguish will be on the land, and wrath and retribution on this people [Israel].
AMP.24: And they will fall by the edge of the sword, and will be led captive into all nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled underfoot by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled (completed).
AMP.25: "There will be signs (attesting miracles) in the sun and moon and stars; and on the earth [there will be] distress and anguish among nations, in perplexity at the roaring and tossing of the sea and the waves,
AMP.26: people fainting from fear and expectation of the [dreadful] things coming on the world; for the [very] powers of the heavens will be shaken.
AMP.27: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with [transcendent, overwhelming] power [subduing the nations] and with great glory.
AMP.28: Now when these things begin to occur, stand tall and lift up your heads [in joy], because [suffering ends as] your redemption is drawing near."
AMP.29: Then He told them a parable: "Look at the fig tree and all the trees;
AMP.30: as soon as they put out leaves, you see it and know for yourselves that summer is near.
AMP.31: So you too, when you see these things happening, know [without any doubt] that the kingdom of God is near.
AMP.32: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, this generation [those living at that definite period of time preceding the second coming] will not pass away until everything takes place.
AMP.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
AMP.34: "But be on guard, so that your hearts are not weighed down and depressed with the giddiness of debauchery and the nausea of self-indulgence and the worldly worries of life, and then that day [when the Messiah returns] will not come on you suddenly like a trap;
AMP.35: for it will come upon all those who live on the face of all the earth.
AMP.36: But keep alert at all times [be attentive and ready], praying that you may have the strength and ability [to be found worthy and] to escape all these things that are going to take place, and to stand in the presence of the Son of Man [at His coming]."
AMP.37: Now in the daytime Jesus was teaching in [the porches and courts of] the temple, but at night He would go out and spend the night on the mount that is called Olivet.
AMP.38: And early in the morning all the people would come to Him in the temple to listen to Him.

MSG.1: Just then he looked up and saw the rich people dropping offerings in the collection plate.
MSG.2: Then he saw a poor widow put in two pennies.
MSG.3: He said, "The plain truth is that this widow has given by far the largest offering today.
MSG.4: All these others made offerings that they'll never miss; she gave extravagantly what she couldn't afford—she gave her all!"
MSG.5: One day people were standing around talking about the Temple, remarking how beautiful it was, the splendor of its stonework and memorial gifts. Jesus said,
MSG.6: "All this you're admiring so much—the time is coming when every stone in that building will end up in a heap of rubble."
MSG.7: They asked him, "Teacher, when is this going to happen? What clue will we get that it's about to take place?"
MSG.8: He said, "Watch out for the doomsday deceivers. Many leaders are going to show up with forged identities claiming, 'I'm the One,' or, 'The end is near.' Don't fall for any of that.
MSG.9: When you hear of wars and uprisings, keep your head and don't panic. This is routine history and no sign of the end."
MSG.10: He went on, "Nation will fight nation and ruler fight ruler, over and over.
MSG.11: Huge earthquakes will occur in various places. There will be famines. You'll think at times that the very sky is falling.
MSG.12: "But before any of this happens, they'll arrest you, hunt you down, and drag you to court and jail. It will go from bad to worse, dog-eat-dog, everyone at your throat because you carry my name.
MSG.13: You'll end up on the witness stand, called to testify.
MSG.14: Make up your mind right now not to worry about it.
MSG.15: I'll give you the words and wisdom that will reduce all your accusers to stammers and stutters.
MSG.16: "You'll even be turned in by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends. Some of you will be killed.
MSG.17: There's no telling who will hate you because of me.
MSG.18: Even so, every detail of your body and soul—even the hairs of your head!—is in my care; nothing of you will be lost.
MSG.19: Staying with it—that's what is required. Stay with it to the end. You won't be sorry; you'll be saved.
MSG.20: "When you see soldiers camped all around Jerusalem, then you'll know that she is about to be devastated.
MSG.21: If you're living in Judea at the time, run for the hills. If you're in the city, get out quickly. If you're out in the fields, don't go home to get your coat.
MSG.22: This is Vengeance Day—everything written about it will come to a head.
MSG.23: Pregnant and nursing mothers will have it especially hard. Incredible misery! Torrential rage!
MSG.24: People dropping like flies; people dragged off to prisons; Jerusalem under the boot of barbarians until the nations finish what was given them to do.
MSG.25: "It will seem like all hell has broken loose—sun, moon, stars, earth, sea,
MSG.26: in an uproar and everyone all over the world in a panic, the wind knocked out of them by the threat of doom, the powers-that-be quaking.
MSG.27: "And then—then!—they'll see the Son of Man welcomed in grand style—a glorious welcome!
MSG.28: When all this starts to happen, up on your feet. Stand tall with your heads high. Help is on the way!"
MSG.29: He told them a story. "Look at a fig tree. Any tree for that matter.
MSG.30: When the leaves begin to show, one look tells you that summer is right around the corner.
MSG.31: The same here—when you see these things happen, you know God's kingdom is about here.
MSG.32: Don't brush this off: I'm not just saying this for some future generation, but for this one, too—these things will happen.
MSG.33: Sky and earth will wear out; my words won't wear out.
MSG.34: "But be on your guard. Don't let the sharp edge of your expectation get dulled by parties and drinking and shopping. Otherwise, that Day is going to take you by complete surprise, spring on you suddenly like a trap,
MSG.35: for it's going to come on everyone, everywhere, at once.
MSG.36: So, whatever you do, don't go to sleep at the switch. Pray constantly that you will have the strength and wits to make it through everything that's coming and end up on your feet before the Son of Man."
MSG.37: He spent his days in the Temple teaching, but his nights out on the mountain called Olives.
MSG.38: All the people were up at the crack of dawn to come to the Temple and listen to him.

NIV.1:
The Widow’s Offering
As he looked up, Jesus saw the rich putting their gifts into the temple treasury.
NIV.2: He also saw a poor widow put in two very small copper coins.
NIV.3: “I tell you the truth,” he said, “this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
NIV.4: All these people gave their gifts out of their wealth; but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on.”
NIV.5:
Signs of the End of the Age
Some of his disciples were remarking about how the temple was adorned with beautiful stones and with gifts dedicated to God. But Jesus said,
NIV.6: “As for what you see here, the time will come when not one stone will be left on another; every one of them will be thrown down.”
NIV.7: “Teacher,” they asked, “when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are about to take place?”
NIV.8: He replied: “Watch out that you are not deceived. For many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is near.’ Do not follow them.
NIV.9: When you hear of wars and revolutions, do not be frightened. These things must happen first, but the end will not come right away.”
NIV.10: Then he said to them: “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
NIV.11: There will be great earthquakes, famines and pestilences in various places, and fearful events and great signs from heaven.
NIV.12: “But before all this, they will lay hands on you and persecute you. They will deliver you to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors, and all on account of my name.
NIV.13: This will result in your being witnesses to them.
NIV.14: But make up your mind not to worry beforehand how you will defend yourselves.
NIV.15: For I will give you words and wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to resist or contradict.
NIV.16: You will be betrayed even by parents, brothers, relatives and friends, and they will put some of you to death.
NIV.17: All men will hate you because of me.
NIV.18: But not a hair of your head will perish.
NIV.19: By standing firm you will gain life.
NIV.20: “When you see Jerusalem being surrounded by armies, you will know that its desolation is near.
NIV.21: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, let those in the city get out, and let those in the country not enter the city.
NIV.22: For this is the time of punishment in fulfillment of all that has been written.
NIV.23: How dreadful it will be in those days for pregnant women and nursing mothers! There will be great distress in the land and wrath against this people.
NIV.24: They will fall by the sword and will be taken as prisoners to all the nations. Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
NIV.25: “There will be signs in the sun, moon and stars. On the earth, nations will be in anguish and perplexity at the roaring and tossing of the sea.
NIV.26: Men will faint from terror, apprehensive of what is coming on the world, for the heavenly bodies will be shaken.
NIV.27: At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
NIV.28: When these things begin to take place, stand up and lift up your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
NIV.29: He told them this parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the trees.
NIV.30: When they sprout leaves, you can see for yourselves and know that summer is near.
NIV.31: Even so, when you see these things happening, you know that the kingdom of God is near.
NIV.32: “I tell you the truth, this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things have happened.
NIV.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
NIV.34: “Be careful, or your hearts will be weighed down with dissipation, drunkenness and the anxieties of life, and that day will close on you unexpectedly like a trap.
NIV.35: For it will come upon all those who live on the face of the whole earth.
NIV.36: Be always on the watch, and pray that you may be able to escape all that is about to happen, and that you may be able to stand before the Son of Man.”
NIV.37: Each day Jesus was teaching at the temple, and each evening he went out to spend the night on the hill called the Mount of Olives,
NIV.38: and all the people came early in the morning to hear him at the temple.

NRSVCE.1: He looked up and saw rich people putting their gifts into the treasury;
NRSVCE.2: he also saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.
NRSVCE.3: He said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all of them;
NRSVCE.4: for all of them have contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”
NRSVCE.5: When some were speaking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and gifts dedicated to God, he said,
NRSVCE.6: “As for these things that you see, the days will come when not one stone will be left upon another; all will be thrown down.”
NRSVCE.7: They asked him, “Teacher, when will this be, and what will be the sign that this is about to take place?”
NRSVCE.8: And he said, “Beware that you are not led astray; for many will come in my name and say, ‘I am he!’ and, ‘The time is near!’ Do not go after them.
NRSVCE.9: “When you hear of wars and insurrections, do not be terrified; for these things must take place first, but the end will not follow immediately.”
NRSVCE.10: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom;
NRSVCE.11: there will be great earthquakes, and in various places famines and plagues; and there will be dreadful portents and great signs from heaven.
NRSVCE.12: “But before all this occurs, they will arrest you and persecute you; they will hand you over to synagogues and prisons, and you will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
NRSVCE.13: This will give you an opportunity to testify.
NRSVCE.14: So make up your minds not to prepare your defense in advance;
NRSVCE.15: for I will give you words and a wisdom that none of your opponents will be able to withstand or contradict.
NRSVCE.16: You will be betrayed even by parents and brothers, by relatives and friends; and they will put some of you to death.
NRSVCE.17: You will be hated by all because of my name.
NRSVCE.18: But not a hair of your head will perish.
NRSVCE.19: By your endurance you will gain your souls.
NRSVCE.20: “When you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.
NRSVCE.21: Then those in Judea must flee to the mountains, and those inside the city must leave it, and those out in the country must not enter it;
NRSVCE.22: for these are days of vengeance, as a fulfillment of all that is written.
NRSVCE.23: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days! For there will be great distress on the earth and wrath against this people;
NRSVCE.24: they will fall by the edge of the sword and be taken away as captives among all nations; and Jerusalem will be trampled on by the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
NRSVCE.25: “There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars, and on the earth distress among nations confused by the roaring of the sea and the waves.
NRSVCE.26: People will faint from fear and foreboding of what is coming upon the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
NRSVCE.27: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in a cloud’ with power and great glory.
NRSVCE.28: Now when these things begin to take place, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
NRSVCE.29: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the trees;
NRSVCE.30: as soon as they sprout leaves you can see for yourselves and know that summer is already near.
NRSVCE.31: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that the kingdom of God is near.
NRSVCE.32: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all things have taken place.
NRSVCE.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
NRSVCE.34: “Be on guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life, and that day does not catch you unexpectedly,
NRSVCE.35: like a trap. For it will come upon all who live on the face of the whole earth.
NRSVCE.36: Be alert at all times, praying that you may have the strength to escape all these things that will take place, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
NRSVCE.37: Every day he was teaching in the temple, and at night he would go out and spend the night on the Mount of Olives, as it was called.
NRSVCE.38: And all the people would get up early in the morning to listen to him in the temple.

NET.1: Jesus looked up and saw the rich putting their gifts into the offering box.
NET.2: He also saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins.
NET.3: He said, “I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put in more than all of them.
NET.4: For they all offered their gifts out of their wealth. But she, out of her poverty, put in everything she had to live on.”
NET.5: Now while some were speaking about the temple, how it was adorned with beautiful stones and offerings, Jesus said,
NET.6: “As for these things that you are gazing at, the days will come when not one stone will be left on another. All will be torn down!”
NET.7: So they asked him, “Teacher, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that these things are about to take place?”
NET.8: He said, “Watch out that you are not misled. For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and, ‘The time is near.’ Do not follow them!
NET.9: And when you hear of wars and rebellions, do not be afraid. For these things must happen first, but the end will not come at once.”
NET.10: Then he said to them, “Nation will rise up in arms against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
NET.11: There will be great earthquakes, and famines and plagues in various places, and there will be terrifying sights and great signs from heaven.
NET.12: But before all this, they will seize you and persecute you, handing you over to the synagogues and prisons. You will be brought before kings and governors because of my name.
NET.13: This will be a time for you to serve as witnesses.
NET.14: Therefore be resolved not to rehearse ahead of time how to make your defense.
NET.15: For I will give you the words along with the wisdom that none of your adversaries will be able to withstand or contradict.
NET.16: You will be betrayed even by parents, brothers, relatives, and friends, and they will have some of you put to death.
NET.17: You will be hated by everyone because of my name.
NET.18: Yet not a hair of your head will perish.
NET.19: By your endurance you will gain your lives.
NET.20: “But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by armies, then know that its desolation has come near.
NET.21: Then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains. Those who are inside the city must depart. Those who are out in the country must not enter it,
NET.22: because these are days of vengeance, to fulfill all that is written.
NET.23: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing their babies in those days! For there will be great distress on the earth and wrath against this people.
NET.24: They will fall by the edge of the sword and be led away as captives among all nations. Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
NET.25: “And there will be signs in the sun and moon and stars, and on the earth nations will be in distress, anxious over the roaring of the sea and the surging waves.
NET.26: People will be fainting from fear and from the expectation of what is coming on the world, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
NET.27: Then they will see the Son of Man arriving in a cloud with power and great glory.
NET.28: But when these things begin to happen, stand up and raise your heads, because your redemption is drawing near.”
NET.29: Then he told them a parable: “Look at the fig tree and all the other trees.
NET.30: When they sprout leaves, you see for yourselves and know that summer is now near.
NET.31: So also you, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
NET.32: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
NET.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
NET.34: “But be on your guard so that your hearts are not weighed down with dissipation and drunkenness and the worries of this life, and that day close down upon you suddenly like a trap.
NET.35: For it will overtake all who live on the face of the whole earth.
NET.36: But stay alert at all times, praying that you may have strength to escape all these things that must happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.”
NET.37: So every day Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, but at night he went and stayed on the Mount of Olives.
NET.38: And all the people came to him early in the morning to listen to him in the temple courts.
